$comment: Hexagonal layer manifest (Issue #32). Single source of truth for the persistence boundary. Consumed by tests/architecture-boundary.test.mjs, which scans import specifiers and fails if any layer's dependency direction rots. Paths are repo-relative POSIX. server.ts is the composition root and is intentionally allowed to import the concrete adapter; splitting it into use-cases is a later cluster.
$compositionNote: server.ts is the process entry/composition root: it holds no business logic and registers no tools directly — it builds the AppContext and delegates to the application layer (src/app/). context.ts is the wiring factory that constructs the concrete Store adapter. Both may import the adapter; the tool modules under src/app/tools/ may not.
$clockNote: The Clock/IdGenerator ports (src/ports/clock.ts) are injected by the composition root (src/app/context.ts) into these consumers; each depends on the port, not on ambient nowIso/newId. Enforced by tests/architecture-boundary.test.mjs.
$domainPureNote: Modules proven free of BOTH I/O imports AND raw store.db access, enforced by tests/architecture-boundary.test.mjs. hypotheses.ts is intentionally excluded: HypothesisRepo is the hypothesis repository (DAO). It now orchestrates persistence exclusively through typed PersistenceStore methods (no raw SQL), but is classified a persistence consumer rather than domain-pure to reflect its repository role. The port exposes no raw SQL gateway; no consumer touches store.db.
